top30.title: Towards autonomous medical artificial intelligence agents
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10675-5A large language model artificial intelligence agent operating in a sandboxed electronic health record system can autonomously take patient histories, order tests, interpret findings, diagnose conditions and propose treatments, outperforming experienced clinicians while adhering to safety standards and clinical guidelines.

top30.title: BRIDGE: benchmarking large language models for understanding real-world clinical practice texts
top30.abstract: Nature Biomedical Engineering, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41551-026-01719-2The BRIDGE benchmark systematically evaluated the performance of 95 large language models (LLMs) on real-world clinical data on tasks spanning the patient care continuum. This benchmark, which is being continuously updated, provides a field reference for improving how LLMs understand clinical texts.

top30.title: A foundation model to help understand the regulatory implications of 3D genome organization
top30.abstract: Nature Methods, Published online: 15 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41592-026-03098-7We developed a foundation model for 3D genome organization from Hi-C-derived chromatin structure. HiCFoundation supports integrative analysis linking genome architecture to downstream regulatory function. Through large-scale self-supervised pretraining on Hi-C data, followed by fine-tuning on downstream tasks, it provides a unified, efficient, generalizable and interpretable paradigm for 3D genome, single-cell and multiomics analysis across species.

top30.title: Visualizing the impact of quenched disorder on 2D electron Wigner solids
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10654-wA technique combining atomically resolved scanning tunnelling microscopy with neural-quantum-state quantum Monte Carlo simulation of disordered 2D electron Wigner solids establishes a powerful framework to enable the clear identification of two distinct defect-induced disorder regimes.

top30.title: Cucurbituril-based anion-conducting membranes with supramolecular nanopores
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10666-6A supramolecular strategy, in which uniform, dynamic nanopores are constructed, overcomes the limitations of limited structural control, mechanical fragility or demanding synthesis in nanoporous anion-conducting membranes, providing a versatile tool for molecularly engineering transport pathways.

top30.title: Leveraging longitudinal data to boost statistical power for gene–environment interaction analysis
top30.abstract: Nature Computational Science, Published online: 16 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s43588-026-01002-zThe authors introduce SAGELD, a method that leverages longitudinal data to detect gene–environment interactions with greater statistical power than conventional approaches based on cross-sectional data, improving discovery in large biobank studies.

top30.title: Flow matching for fast, multi-purpose structure-based ligand generation for drug discovery
top30.abstract: Nature Computational Science, Published online: 02 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s43588-026-00997-9We developed FLOWR, a structure-aware multi-purpose generative model unifying de novo ligand generation, fragment-based and interaction-conditional sampling. We demonstrated that FLOWR generates ligands up to 70-fold faster compared with existing methods while improving the physical validity and interaction accuracy of generated compounds.

top30.title: A generalizable Hi-C foundation model for chromatin architecture, single-cell and multiomics analysis across species
top30.abstract: Nature Methods, Published online: 15 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41592-026-03097-8HiCFoundation is a foundation model pretrained on a large corpus of Hi-C data for comprehensive 3D genome and epigenomics analysis.

top30.title: Methane storage using metal–dipyrazolate frameworks
top30.abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 18 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02621-7Reversible phase transformations enable flexible metal–bipyrazolate frameworks to offer high deliverable methane capacities, high stability and recyclability, indicating utility in natural gas storage.

top30.title: Author Correction: Autophagic cell death restricts chromosomal instability during replicative crisis
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 19 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10812-0Author Correction: Autophagic cell death restricts chromosomal instability during replicative crisis

top30.title: A long-lived butterfly’s secret to graceful ageing
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 19 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01948-0Species in the Heliconius genus are among the longest-lived butterflies, thanks to a diet of pollen.

top30.title: Briefing Chat: Testosterone and sperm might get a boost from obesity drugs
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 19 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01963-1Nature staff discuss preliminary data on the effects of GLP-1 drugs on male fertility, plus a two-year trial of a brain–computer interface.

top30.title: Stem cells banish severe autoimmune disease for 15 years
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 19 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01925-7Two people were the first to receive the therapy for a condition that damages the spinal cord and optic nerve.

top30.title: Clues to the sloth’s sloth found in its genome
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 18 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01869-ySequencing shows duplication of genes that affect mitochondria, the organelles that provide energy for cells.

top30.title: It slices! It dices! Sashimi-Bot handles seafood with ease
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 18 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01871-4Fillets of raw fish are irregular in shape and floppy in texture, but a robot with three arms has learnt how to manipulate a salmon loin.

top30.title: Is AI ruining our skills? Early results are in — and they’re not good
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 18 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01947-1Reliance on artificial-intelligence tools degrades the abilities of physicians and software engineers, studies show.

top30.title: Brexit tore apart European science — now the research rifts are healing
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 18 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01841-wThe UK’s share of EU research funding is climbing, but lost networks will be harder to recover.

top30.title: Cell transplant across the tree of life hints at how animals emerged
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 18 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01910-0Embryonic ‘organizer cells’ can tell embryos of various phyla what kind of body to build.

top30.title: Daily briefing: The brain builds a sentence neuron by neuron
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 18 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01967-xResearchers have tracked the electrical activity of individual brain cells during conversation in real time. Plus, the history of GPS and a cross-species transplant that could reveal clues about the origin of animals.

top30.title: Towards Conversational AI for Disease Management
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10764-5Towards Conversational AI for Disease Management

top30.title: The brain region that could provide a cognitive ‘reservoir’ in old age
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01870-5Having a greater tissue volume in some parts of the cerebellum is linked to higher scores on cognitive tests.

top30.title: DNA from hunter-gatherer teeth reveals secrets of ancient plague
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01941-7Analysis shows evidence of deadly outbreaks 5,500 years ago — plus, a prototype dark-matter detector.

top30.title: Emergent decadal predictability in Antarctic contribution to sea-level rise
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10614-4Although Antarctic ice loss projections diverge widely by 2100, this Perspective shows that present-day rates robustly predict mid-century sea level rise, providing a firm basis for near-term planning, while highlighting priorities for model development aimed at resolving longer-term sea level rise uncertainty.

top30.title: A distant brown dwarf coplanar to a warm Jupiter and a hot super-Earth
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10586-5Analysis of long-term radial velocity data and transit time variations, induced by a super-Earth, a warm Jupiter and a brown dwarf in a coplanar orbit around the relatively young star TOI201.

top30.title: Probing picometre-scale interlayer deformations via hyperbolic polaritons
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10638-wA new polaritonic optical method that uses the mid-infrared out-of-plane hyperbolic polaritons mode is described and experimentally validated to allow the examination of picometre-scale interlayer deformations, providing a bridge between nanomechanics and photonics.

top30.title: Optical fibre gripper for high-performance 3D micromanipulation
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10673-7A miniature three-dimensional optical fibre gripper enables powerful, precise micromanipulation of particles and single cells in confined spaces, bridging the gap between optical and mechanical tweezers.

top30.title: A prototype differential atom interferometer for fundamental physics
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10617-1A prototype differential atom interferometer operates at the standard quantum limit with no excess noise beyond atom shot noise, achieving performance in line with the specifications for future long-baseline atom interferometers.

top30.title: Structure of the pre-initiation complex explains CMGE biogenesis
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10657-7Cryo-electron microscopy and biochemical reconstitution experiments in yeast provide insight into the assembly of the CMGE complex, a helicase that establishes bidirectional DNA replication in eukaryotic cells, and elucidate the role of the firing factor Sld2.

top30.title: A 98-qubit trapped-ion quantum computer with all-to-all connectivity
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10676-4A new quantum computer, Quantinuum Helios, which is a 98-qubit trapped-ion quantum processor built on the QCCD architecture, demonstrates performance well beyond classical capabilities and provides a path for scaling up quantum computing.

top30.title: Fast formation to reinforce lithium-rich cathodes
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09553-3Fast formation in lithium-ion batteries outperforms conventional slow formation, lowering costs and improving battery capacity, stability and cycle life, offering broader application to electrode systems.
